rules.logic: The LUF Requirements function as part of a pathway to certification within Gold Standard for the Global Goals, enabling eligible forestry and agriculture activities to undergo Design and Performance Certification.
rules.title: LUF pathway to certification

rules.logic: LUF Projects shall apply these Requirements in conjunction with the Principles & Requirements and GHG Emissions Reduction & Sequestration Product Requirements to issue GS VERs.
rules.text: Conjunction with P&R and Product Requirements
rules.title: Conjunction with P&R and Product Requirements

rules.logic: A/R projects can include: planting trees, single-species plantations, all silvicultural systems (conservation forests, selective harvesting, rotation forestry), and agriculture (agroforestry) or pasture (silvopasture).
rules.title: A/R activity types

rules.logic: AGR projects include eligible activities covered by an approved Gold Standard SDG Impact Quantification methodology.
rules.title: AGR activity types

rules.logic: AGR projects shall hold FSC certification valid throughout the crediting period.
rules.title: FSC dual certification

rules.logic: Project Developer shall: own CO2 user rights, hold uncontested legal land title, own timber/non-timber forest product rights, hold all necessary permits.
rules.title: A/R secured titles

rules.logic: Where PD acts on behalf: each participant signs agreement confirming CO2 user rights passed to PD, rights to implement activities, uncontested land title.
rules.text: AGR secured titles - developer on behalf
rules.title: AGR secured titles - developer on behalf

rules.logic: Where PD acts alone: PD holds CO2 user rights, rights to implement activities, uncontested land title.
rules.text: AGR secured titles - developer acts alone
rules.title: AGR secured titles - developer acts alone

rules.logic: New areas added after Registered status via Design Certification process. VVB site visit and opinion required.
rules.title: New area addition

rules.logic: If host country accounts for land-based activities nationally, projects must conform to GHG Product Requirements Annex A Double Counting.
rules.title: Double counting prevention

rules.logic: Preserving adaptive capacity integral to every AGR project. Demonstrated via training, knowledge sharing, livelihood diversification, soil fertility improvement.
rules.title: Climate resilience - AGR

rules.logic: PD conducts Safeguarding Principles assessment per Risks & Capacities Guideline. Submitted Preliminary Review, updated for Design and Performance Certification.
rules.title: Safeguarding assessment

rules.logic: Minimum 10% of total Project Area for biodiversity protection following HCV approach: native tree patches, solitary stems, endangered species habitats, connectivity areas.
rules.title: 10% biodiversity protection

rules.logic: 15-meter buffer on both sides of water bodies. In buffer: keep native trees, no fertilizer/pesticides, no logging, no heavy machinery, no cropping. Planted trees must be native.
rules.title: Water body buffer zone

rules.logic: Stakeholder Consultation before project start date. Refer to Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements.
rules.title: Stakeholder consultation timing

rules.logic: AGR: first within 2 years. A/R: every 5 years. All: crediting renewal every 5th year with baseline update.
rules.title: Verification frequency

rules.logic: Option 1: Apply A/R CDM Combined tool for baseline and additionality. CDM terms interpreted in Gold Standard context.
rules.title: Additionality - CDM tool A/R

rules.logic: Option 1: Apply CDM standardized baselines guidelines or Combined tool.
rules.title: Additionality - CDM tool AGR

rules.logic: Option 2: Must meet LDC/low HDI, not mandatory, degraded land, plus one of: native species, community benefit, 5+ species in 50%+ area, HDI<0.5 or SIDS.
rules.title: Additionality - positive list A/R

rules.logic: Option 2: HDI<=0.7, not mandatory/enforced, plus one of: <600mm rain, 5+ native crop species, smallholder <5% adoption, HDI<0.5 or SIDS.
rules.title: Additionality - positive list AGR

rules.logic: Maximum 3 years retroactive, from Project Start Date or 3 years before Design Certification, whichever is later.
rules.title: Retroactive issuance limit

rules.logic: All projects submit GIS layers: Project Region, Project Area, Eligible Areas, Modelling Units, Infrastructure, Land use, Water bodies, Carbon pools.
rules.title: GIS vector layers

rules.logic: A/R: 1 GPS per 5ha with hand-drawn map. AGR: 1 GPS per participant with map. Protected areas via UNESCO/national park maps.
rules.title: GPS and mapping

rules.logic: Target precision: 20% of mean at 90% confidence level for all GHG estimates.
rules.title: Uncertainty target

rules.logic: Approach 1: on-site measurements. Approach 2: peer-reviewed literature. Approach 3: default factors with deduction if threshold not met.
rules.title: Three uncertainty approaches

rules.logic: If uncertainty >20%: 20-30% → 50% UD, 30-40% → 75% UD, 40-50% → 100% UD. No credits if >50%.
rules.title: Uncertainty deduction table
